title: Macleaya jedoensis
taxonomy: Macleaya jedoensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Papaveraceae. This family includes plants commonly known as poppies and their relatives. Macleaya jedoensis is a genus of flowering plants native to East Asia. The plant is often used as an ornamental in gardens and landscapes due to its unique foliage and tall spikes of white or pink flowers. Macleaya jedoensis is sometimes referred to as the white plume poppy.
uses: Macleaya jedoensis is known for its traditional use in Korean medicine. Its roots and leaves are used to treat various conditions, including fever and rheumatism.
